channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop’s workflows

item.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI-Assisted Coding
item.description: Three LLM agents generated 600,000+ lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle playground competition. Demonstrates how agentic coding plus GPU acceleration compresses the ML development cycle from weeks to days.

item.title: A pelican for GPT-5.5 via the Codex backdoor API
item.description: Simon Willison builds an llm-openai-via-codex plugin to access GPT-5.5 through the Codex subscriber API before the official API launch. Finds it highly capable but notes the model's default output is less polished than extended-reasoning mode.

item.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure
item.description: NVIDIA deploys GPT-5.5-powered Codex company-wide on GB200 NVL72 systems with 10,000+ employees using it. Debugging cycles reduced from days to hours — enterprise proof-of-concept for frontier model plus specialised inference hardware.

item.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
item.description: Anthropic confirms and resolves three issues degrading Claude Code quality since March 2026: reduced default reasoning effort, a caching bug causing context loss, and a verbosity-reduction prompt hurting coding performance. All three now fixed.

item.title: Meta Plans to Cut 10% of Workers to Offset AI Spending
item.description: Meta will cut ~8,000 jobs (10% of workforce) on May 20 and leave 6,000 roles unfilled to offset AI infrastructure costs. AI is writing 4x more code than engineers at Meta — layoffs framed explicitly as an AI-productivity dividend.

item.title: Introducing GPT-5.5
item.description: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5, its most capable model, optimised for agentic coding, computer use, and long-context scientific research. Scores 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 and 85% on ARC-AGI-2 while matching GPT-5.4 latency. Priced at $5/1M input and $30/1M output — double GPT-5.4 pricing.

item.title: GPT-5.5 System Card
item.description: OpenAI's safety documentation for GPT-5.5 covers capability evaluations including Terminal-Bench 2.0 and ARC-AGI-2, biosecurity mitigations, and preparedness framework updates. Published alongside the model launch to satisfy responsible scaling commitments.

item.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
item.description: Meta introduces Muse Spark, a personal AI system that continuously builds a detailed model of each user's goals, preferences, and context — designed to grow more capable and personalised over time. Meta's clearest statement yet on the path to individual-scale AI.

item.title: Microsoft doubles NZ AI upskilling commitment to train 200,000 Kiwis
item.description: Microsoft has doubled its New Zealand AI upskilling target to 200,000 people, expanding beyond basic digital literacy to real-world AI skills. Announced by Satya Nadella, targeting workforce readiness for AI-driven productivity.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
item.description: OpenAI opens a red-teaming challenge targeting GPT-5.5's biosecurity guardrails, offering up to $25,000 for researchers who find universal jailbreaks for bio-related safety risks. Part of the responsible release process for a frontier model at higher capability thresholds.

item.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
item.description: NVIDIA details running Gemma 4 locally on RTX PCs and the DGX Spark desktop, including supported open models in the RTX AI Garage. Positions local agentic AI on consumer hardware as a practical path for developers and enterprises wanting on-premise inference.

item.title: The Future of AI Is Open and Proprietary
item.description: NVIDIA argues the AI landscape will be defined by open and proprietary models coexisting — open weights enabling customisation and sovereignty while closed frontier models deliver maximum raw capability. A perspective piece from a key infrastructure player on AI's structural future.

item.title: How Autonomous AI Agents Become Secure by Design With NVIDIA OpenShell
item.description: NVIDIA outlines how the OpenShell agent runtime enforces security-by-design for autonomous AI agents, covering sandboxing, policy enforcement, and auditability. Addresses a critical production gap as enterprises move from demos to live agentic deployments.

item.title: Blowing Off Steam: How Power-Flexible AI Factories Can Stabilize the Global Energy Grid
item.description: NVIDIA proposes that AI data centres with power-flexible workloads could serve as grid stabilisers — absorbing surplus renewable energy during off-peak periods and throttling during grid stress. Frames large-scale AI infrastructure as a potential ally in energy grid management.

item.title: From Simulation to Production: How to Build Robots With AI
item.description: NVIDIA guide covering the full pipeline from simulation-based robot training to production deployment using Isaac Lab, Omniverse, and foundation models. Practical reference for teams building physical AI systems across manufacturing, logistics, and service robotics.

item.title: Introducing Grok Business and Grok Enterprise
item.description: xAI announced Grok Business and Grok Enterprise tiers with team management, higher usage limits, and enterprise-grade controls. Positions Grok as a direct competitor to OpenAI Teams and Anthropic's enterprise offerings in the commercial AI market.

item.title: xAI partners with US Department of War to apply Grok to military applications
item.description: xAI announced a partnership with the US Department of War to deploy Grok for military applications, joining other frontier labs with government defence contracts. Adds xAI to the growing roster of AI companies providing capabilities to national security programmes.

item.title: xAI and El Salvador Pioneer the World's First Nationwide AI Education Program
item.description: xAI and the El Salvador government launched what they describe as the world's first nationwide AI education programme, using Grok to deliver AI literacy across the country's education system. Positions xAI as both a commercial and geopolitical actor in global AI adoption.

item.title: Grok 4.1 Fast and Agent Tools API
item.description: xAI released Grok 4.1 Fast, an optimised variant tuned for lower latency in production pipelines, alongside an Agent Tools API enabling external tool calls and multi-step reasoning. Targets developers building real-time agentic applications requiring fast inference.

item.title: Grok goes Global with KSA
item.description: xAI expanded Grok access to Saudi Arabia as part of a broader global rollout, partnering with local entities to provide AI capabilities regionally. Part of xAI's push to compete internationally against OpenAI and Anthropic beyond the US market.

item.title: Expanding xAI for Government with GSA OneGov
item.description: xAI expanded its government programme through a partnership with the GSA's OneGov platform, making Grok available to US federal agencies through existing procurement channels. Builds on the initial xAI for Government announcement with a concrete federal acquisition pathway.

item.title: Announcing xAI for Government
item.description: xAI formally announced a government programme bringing Grok to federal and state agencies, emphasising security, compliance, and enterprise controls. Positions xAI as a direct competitor to Microsoft/OpenAI and Google in the government AI market.

item.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
item.description: Meta describes its internal methodology for scaling AI development and testing, including evaluation frameworks, red-teaming processes, and infrastructure for assessing increasingly capable models. Adds transparency to Meta's AI safety and capability evaluation approach.

item.title: How Alta Daily Uses Meta's Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
item.description: Fashion startup Alta Daily uses Meta's Segment Anything Model to automatically extract and categorise clothing items from photos for a digital wardrobe app. A commercial use case demonstrating SAM's reach into consumer product applications beyond research.

item.title: SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking
item.description: Meta released SAM 3.1, adding multiplexing (simultaneous multi-object tracking) and global reasoning to its Segment Anything Model for real-time video. Faster and more accessible than SAM 3.0, with improved accuracy in complex scenes.

item.title: Four MTIA Chips in Two Years: Scaling AI Experiences for Billions
item.description: Meta released its fourth MTIA (Meta Training and Inference Accelerator) chip in two years, each generation purpose-built for scaling AI inference at the billions-of-users level. Demonstrates Meta's aggressive pace in custom silicon alongside its open-model strategy.

item.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
item.description: Google and NVIDIA demonstrate Gemma 4 running as a Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model on the Jetson Orin Nano Super, enabling on-device robot perception and planning. Gemma 4 proves capable as a lightweight foundation model for physical AI applications on constrained edge hardware.

item.title: Google launches eighth-generation TPUs: 8T for training, 8I for inference
item.description: Google unveiled two specialized eighth-generation TPUs at Cloud Next 2026 — the 8T (training) and 8I (inference) — each delivering 2-3x performance over the previous generation and purpose-built for the agentic era. Logan Kilpatrick (Google AI Studio) called them 'Google's secret sauce' now available through Cloud.

item.title: Google invests in its first data center in Austria
item.description: Google opens its first Austrian data center in Kronstorf, creating 100 direct jobs and expanding European AI compute capacity. Part of the ongoing global infrastructure build-out to meet surging AI demand.

item.title: From Rainforests to Recycling Plants: 5 Ways NVIDIA AI Is Protecting the Planet
item.description: NVIDIA's Earth Day roundup covers five production deployments of AI-accelerated computing across climate modelling, biodiversity conservation, disaster monitoring, and recycling-plant automation. Documents real deployments rather than research demos.

item.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate to Advance Agentic and Physical AI
item.description: NVIDIA and Google Cloud deepened their decade-long joint platform spanning every technology layer from optimised libraries to enterprise cloud services, enabling developers to push agentic and physical AI from lab to production. The collaboration targets robotics, autonomous systems, and large-scale agent deployment pipelines.

item.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
item.description: DeepMind introduces Decoupled DiLoCo, a distributed training method separating gradient computation from parameter updates for improved resilience on heterogeneous hardware. Enables efficient LLM training across commodity clusters without tight synchronisation.

item.title: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
item.description: OpenAI details how WebSocket connections and connection-scoped caching in the Responses API reduced API overhead and improved model latency inside the Codex agent loop. A practical deep dive for builders running long-horizon agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: NZ AI skills demand quadruples; older workers outperforming juniors on AI adoption
item.description: Demand for AI-related skills in New Zealand has quadrupled in job ads over the past decade, and workers over 55 are outperforming younger colleagues in AI adoption rates. Despite 44% of NZ workers using AI daily, only 13% have received formal training — a gap Microsoft aims to close with 200,000 upskilling spots.

item.title: NZ workers not actively choosing an AI future — just clicking accept
item.description: A Newsroom analysis argues New Zealanders are defaulting into an AI-dependent future through passive consent clicks rather than deliberate policy choices. Frames AI adoption as a governance gap — organisations and individuals face AI exposure they have not consciously evaluated.

item.title: Simplify Sparse Deep Learning with Universal Sparse Tensor in nvmath-python
item.description: NVIDIA expands nvmath-python with a Universal Sparse Tensor API that decouples sparsity from memory layout, simplifying sparse model training and inference. Practical tooling for ML engineers optimising large models for efficiency.

item.title: AI is handling 1,000+ NZ job interviews a month — humans still carry all the legal risk
item.description: AI tools are conducting over 1,000 job interviews per month in New Zealand, but legal and ethical accountability remains entirely with human hiring managers. Examines the widening gap between AI deployment in HR and the employment law frameworks governing those decisions.

item.title: OpenAI workspace agents launch — shared agents for multi-step workflows across tools and teams
item.description: Sam Altman endorsed OpenAI's new workspace agents in ChatGPT, calling them 'cool' and predicting most companies will want them. Greg Brockman confirmed they run on cloud-hosted Codex infrastructure with Slack integration and support for recurring long-running tasks.

item.title: Employment Hero launches HeroForce: AI co-employment model for NZ SMEs
item.description: Employment Hero launched HeroForce, an AI-powered co-employment model designed to simplify payroll, HR admin, and employment law compliance for New Zealand SMEs. Targets the country's complex regulatory environment as a commercial obstacle being solved through AI automation.

item.title: Introducing OpenAI Privacy Filter
item.description: OpenAI released an open-weight model for detecting and redacting personally identifiable information (PII) in text, claiming state-of-the-art accuracy on standard benchmarks. Designed for privacy-preserving pipelines that need to scrub user data before forwarding to LLMs.
